title: Mandate secure Kubernetes secret management and eliminate secrets in Git
description: Implement a secure, auditable secret management solution for all Kubernetes secrets. Remove the values-secrets.yaml.example pattern and prevent any secrets or placeholders from being committed to Git. Enforce pre-commit/CI checks to block accidental secret exposure. Adopt either Bitnami Sealed Secrets or External Secrets Operator (ESO) with a cloud secrets manager (e.g., AWS Secrets Manager, HashiCorp Vault, or Cloudflare Workers KV). Ensure all secret injection is handled via CI/CD or sealed/ESO workflow, and document the process for all developers.
subtasks: Remove values-secrets.yaml.example and any base64-encoded secret placeholders from the repository.
Adopt and configure either Bitnami Sealed Secrets or External Secrets Operator (ESO) for all secret management.
If using Sealed Secrets: install ClusterSealedSecretsController, create SealedSecret CRs for all 9 API secrets, and commit only encrypted SealedSecrets to Git.
If using ESO: configure ESO CRs to reference secrets from a cloud secrets manager, and ensure no secret values are present in Git.
Add a git pre-commit hook and CI check (e.g., detect-secrets) to block commits containing any secret keys (e.g., STRIPE_SECRET_KEY, OPENAI_API_KEY, JWT_SECRET).
Update developer documentation to require secret injection via CI/CD or sealed/ESO workflow.
Test secret rotation and injection in a non-production environment.
Run a simulated commit of a file containing a known secret and verify the pre-commit/CI check blocks the commit.
acceptance_criteria: No secret values or base64-encoded placeholders are present in any committed files.
All Kubernetes secrets are managed via Sealed Secrets or ESO and are not present in plaintext in the repository.
Pre-commit and CI checks block any attempt to commit files containing secret keys or values.
Secret rotation can be performed without direct Git changes.
A simulated secret commit is blocked by the pre-commit/CI check.
All services successfully start with secrets injected via the new workflow.
Run Trivy or detect-secrets scan on the repo and confirm no secrets are detected.

title: Enforce strong authentication and audit controls for GDPR internal endpoints
description: Replace header-based authentication on all /internal/gdpr/* endpoints with cryptographically strong service-to-service authentication. Implement mutual TLS (mTLS) or short-lived signed JWTs for internal calls, enforce rate limiting, and ensure all GDPR delete operations are logged with operator identity and timestamp before execution. Add a mandatory human confirmation step for GDPR delete via Morgan's admin skill.
subtasks: Implement mTLS between all internal services using SPIFFE/SPIRE or equivalent, or configure JWT-based authentication with per-service audience claims for GDPR endpoints.
Update Cilium NetworkPolicy to require mTLS or JWT for all /internal/gdpr/* traffic.
Add rate limiting to GDPR delete endpoints (max 5 calls/hour per operator).
Log every GDPR delete invocation with operator identity, timestamp, and customer_id to the audit schema before deletion.
Update Morgan's GDPR delete tool to require explicit human confirmation before execution.
Test that a compromised pod cannot invoke GDPR delete on other services without valid mTLS/JWT.
Verify audit logs are written before any deletion occurs.
Run integration tests simulating GDPR delete requests with and without valid credentials.
acceptance_criteria: All /internal/gdpr/* endpoints require mTLS or signed JWTs for access.
Rate limiting is enforced on GDPR delete endpoints.
Audit logs for GDPR deletes include operator identity, timestamp, and customer_id, and are written before deletion.
Morgan's GDPR delete tool requires human confirmation before proceeding.
Unauthorized or over-limit requests are rejected with appropriate error codes.
Integration tests confirm only authorized, confirmed requests succeed.

title: Redesign JWT authentication architecture for key isolation and rotation
description: Replace the shared HS256 JWT secret with an RS256 asymmetric signing architecture. Deploy a lightweight identity provider (e.g., Keycloak, Dex, or custom JWKS endpoint) to issue tokens with per-service audience claims. Each service must validate the 'aud' claim and use the public key from the JWKS endpoint. Implement key rotation and per-service audience validation. Update Morgan MCP tools to obtain and use service JWTs for all backend calls.
subtasks: Deploy and configure an identity provider (Keycloak, Dex, or custom JWKS endpoint) to issue RS256-signed JWTs.
Update all services to validate JWTs using the public key from the JWKS endpoint and enforce per-service audience claims.
Remove use of the shared HS256 JWT_SECRET; if HS256 is required for v1, use per-service secrets and implement a JWKS-compatible rotation mechanism.
Implement a key rotation process and document it.
Update Morgan MCP tools to obtain and include a valid service JWT in all HTTP calls to backend services.
Document the token refresh strategy for Morgan to handle JWT expiry.
Test that tokens issued for one service are not valid for others.
Run integration tests for token issuance, validation, rotation, and revocation.
acceptance_criteria: All services validate JWTs using RS256 and the JWKS endpoint.
Tokens include per-service audience claims and are rejected by services if the audience does not match.
Morgan MCP tools successfully authenticate to all backend services using JWTs.
Key rotation can be performed without downtime.
No service accepts a token issued for another service.
Integration tests for token validation, rotation, and revocation pass.

title: Harden Stripe webhook endpoint and middleware ordering for secure HMAC validation
description: Ensure the Stripe webhook endpoint processes the raw request body before any middleware deserialization. Register the /api/v1/webhooks/stripe route on a separate router branch that bypasses JWT middleware and uses a dedicated body extractor. Add integration tests to verify correct HMAC validation and route exclusion logic.
subtasks: Refactor route registration so /api/v1/webhooks/stripe is not wrapped by JWT middleware.
Use a dedicated body extractor (e.g., axum::body::Bytes) for the Stripe webhook handler.
Add integration tests: (1) valid Stripe-Signature with no JWT returns 200, (2) HMAC validation uses exact raw bytes.
Document the middleware ordering and exclusion logic.
Test that other /api/v1/* routes remain protected by JWT middleware.
Run OWASP ZAP scan against the webhook endpoint to verify no authentication bypass.
acceptance_criteria: /api/v1/webhooks/stripe is not protected by JWT middleware and processes raw body bytes.
HMAC validation passes for valid Stripe signatures and fails for tampered payloads.
Integration tests for both valid and invalid cases pass.
Other /api/v1/* routes remain protected by JWT middleware.
OWASP ZAP scan shows no authentication bypass or deserialization vulnerabilities.

title: Enforce container image pinning, scanning, and registry trust for all deployments
description: Pin all container images (including signal-cli) to specific digests. Add Trivy or Grype container scanning to the CI pipeline as a required gate. Mirror approved images to a private registry and enforce image policy admission controls to block ':latest' tags and unregistered registries.
subtasks: Update all Deployment manifests to use image digests (e.g., signalapp/signal-cli@sha256:<digest>) instead of tags.
Add Trivy or Grype scanning to the CI pipeline for all images, failing the build on HIGH/CRITICAL findings.
Mirror all production images to a private registry (e.g., ghcr.io/sigma1/ or Cloudflare registry).
Deploy an image policy admission controller (Kyverno or OPA Gatekeeper) to block ':latest' tags and images from unregistered registries.
Apply the image policy to all namespaces, including signal.
Test deployment of a pod with ':latest' tag and verify it is rejected.
Run a full CI pipeline and confirm image scanning is enforced.
acceptance_criteria: All container images are pinned to specific digests in manifests.
CI pipeline fails if Trivy/Grype finds HIGH/CRITICAL vulnerabilities.
No pod can be deployed with ':latest' tag or from an unregistered registry.
All images are pulled from the approved private registry.
Admission controller blocks non-compliant images.
CI pipeline and admission controller tests pass.

title: Enable Valkey (Redis) authentication and enforce secure connection patterns
description: Enable authentication for Valkey (Redis) in all environments, including development. Store the password in a dedicated Kubernetes secret and update all connection strings. Add a Kyverno policy to block unauthenticated or non-TLS Valkey connections if supported.
subtasks: Configure Valkey/Redis to require authentication (requirepass) in all environments.
Store the Valkey password in a Kubernetes secret (sigma1-valkey-secret).
Update VALKEY_SIGMA1_URL in sigma1-infra-endpoints to include the password.
Update all service deployments to use the new connection string.
If supported, enable TLS for Valkey and enforce via Kyverno policy.
Add a Kyverno policy to block Valkey connections without authentication or TLS.
Test that unauthenticated connections are rejected (NOAUTH error).
Run integration tests for all services to confirm they can connect with the new credentials.
acceptance_criteria: Valkey requires authentication in all environments.
All services use the updated connection string with password.
Unauthenticated connections are rejected.
If TLS is enabled, all connections use TLS.
Kyverno policy blocks non-compliant connections.
Integration tests for all services pass with the new configuration.

title: Convert Morgan to StatefulSet with persistent Signal-CLI state
description: Convert the Morgan deployment to a StatefulSet with a dedicated PVC for Signal-CLI state. Ensure Signal-CLI registration data is persisted and survives pod restarts. Add a liveness probe for Signal-CLI JSON-RPC connectivity and document a recovery runbook.
subtasks: Refactor Morgan's Kubernetes manifest from Deployment to StatefulSet.
Configure a volumeClaimTemplate for persistent storage of Signal-CLI state.
Ensure the correct PVC is mounted at the Signal-CLI data path.
Add a liveness probe that checks Signal-CLI JSON-RPC connectivity.
Document a recovery runbook for Signal re-registration.
Test pod restarts and verify Signal-CLI state is preserved.
Simulate a node drain or OOM kill and confirm no data loss.
acceptance_criteria: Morgan runs as a StatefulSet with a dedicated PVC for Signal-CLI state.
Signal-CLI registration data persists across pod restarts.
Liveness probe verifies Signal-CLI JSON-RPC is healthy.
Recovery runbook is documented and tested.
Pod restarts do not result in data loss.

title: Harden ArgoCD Application CRs to prevent accidental pruning of stateful resources
description: Add resource exclusion rules to all ArgoCD Application CRs to prevent pruning of critical stateful resources (PVCs, CloudNative-PG Cluster CRs, Valkey CRs). Implement a two-step sync approval for destructive changes.
subtasks: Update each ArgoCD Application spec to include syncOptions: ["PrunePropagationPolicy=foreground", "PruneLast=true"].
Annotate critical resources with 'argocd.argoproj.io/managed-by-cluster-argocd: false' or use ArgoCD's resource exclusion config.
Implement a manual sync approval gate for destructive changes (e.g., Deployment deletions, replica count reductions).
Test that accidental deletion of a Helm template does not prune PVCs or database clusters.
Document the exclusion rules and sync approval process.
acceptance_criteria: Critical stateful resources are not pruned by ArgoCD automated sync.
Manual approval is required for destructive changes.
Tested deletion of a resource from Git does not result in loss of PVCs or database clusters.
Documentation is updated to reflect the new process.

title: Implement centralized audit logging for all administrative actions
description: Enable Kubernetes API server audit logging and consolidate all GDPR and admin audit entries into a single 'audit' schema. Implement a structured audit log format and add Prometheus alerting on unusual admin activity.
subtasks: Enable Kubernetes API server audit logging with a policy capturing all admin-level events (secret reads, pod exec, deployment modifications).
Ship audit logs to a centralized Loki or OpenSearch instance.
Consolidate all GDPR audit entries into a single 'audit' schema across services.
Implement a structured audit log format: {timestamp, operator_id, action, resource_type, resource_id, service, outcome}.
Add Prometheus alerting for unusual admin API call rates.
Update Grafana dashboards to include an audit events panel.
Test audit log ingestion and cross-service reporting.
acceptance_criteria: All admin actions are logged centrally with structured fields.
GDPR audit entries are consolidated in the 'audit' schema.
Prometheus alerts fire on abnormal admin activity.
Grafana dashboards display audit events.
Audit logs are queryable for compliance reporting.

title: Enforce file upload validation and limits on social engine endpoints
description: Add explicit file validation to the social engine upload handler: check magic bytes for image formats, enforce file size and count limits, sanitize file names, and validate Content-Type. Set S3/R2 bucket policy for object size and implement prompt injection detection for OpenAI vision calls.
subtasks: Implement magic byte validation for JPEG/PNG/WebP using a library like 'file-type'.
Enforce a maximum file size of 25MB per file and a maximum of 20 files per request.
Sanitize file names before using them as R2 keys.
Validate Content-Type on each file part in the multipart body.
Set S3/R2 bucket policy to enforce maximum object size.
Implement strict JSON schema validation on OpenAI vision API responses to detect prompt injection.
Test uploads with invalid file types, oversized files, and excessive file counts.
Run OWASP ZAP scan against the upload endpoint.
acceptance_criteria: Only valid image files (JPEG/PNG/WebP) are accepted.
File size and count limits are enforced.
File names are sanitized and do not allow path traversal or injection.
R2 bucket policy enforces object size.
OpenAI vision responses are strictly validated.
OWASP ZAP scan shows no file upload or injection vulnerabilities.

title: Restrict CORS origins and add origin validation for Morgan web chat
description: Set CORS origins for the Morgan web chat adapter to the specific production domains. Add an Origin validation middleware, implement session-based rate limiting per origin, and add a Cloudflare WAF rule to block unauthorized origins.
subtasks: Update Morgan web chat adapter configuration to set cors_origins to the approved domain list.
Implement Origin validation middleware that returns 403 for unexpected origins.
Implement session-based rate limiting per origin.
Add a Cloudflare WAF rule to block requests to /chat from unapproved Referer headers.
Test cross-origin requests from unauthorized domains are blocked.
Run a security scan to verify CORS and origin validation.
acceptance_criteria: Only approved domains are allowed by CORS and Origin validation.
Session-based rate limiting is enforced per origin.
Cloudflare WAF blocks unauthorized requests.
Security scan confirms CORS and origin validation are effective.

title: Resolve currency provider selection and secure API key management for finance service
description: Select and document a reliable currency rate provider. Add the provider API key to a Kubernetes secret. Enable Redis TLS for financial data caching if supported, and implement a staleness check for cached rates.
subtasks: Select a currency rate provider (e.g., Open Exchange Rates, Fixer.io, ECB XML feed) and document the choice.
Add the provider API key to sigma1-finance-secret.
Update the finance service to use the selected provider and key.
Enable Redis TLS for Valkey if supported by the operator.
Implement a staleness check in the health endpoint for currency rates.
Test rate sync and fallback behavior on provider failure.
acceptance_criteria: A reliable currency provider is selected and documented.
API key is stored securely in a Kubernetes secret.
Finance service uses the new provider and key.
Redis TLS is enabled if supported.
Health endpoint warns if rates are stale.
Tests for rate sync and staleness pass.

title: Enforce dependency auditing and version pinning in CI for all services
description: Move dependency auditing to the earliest CI step for all Go, Rust, and Node.js services. Pin all dependency versions, add audit steps to service-level tests, and configure Dependabot or Renovate for automated security patch PRs.
subtasks: Pin all dependency versions exactly (no caret or tilde ranges) in Cargo.toml, go.mod, and package.json files.
Add 'cargo audit', 'npm audit --audit-level high', and 'govulncheck ./...' to each service's test workflow.
Create a reusable GitHub Actions workflow 'dependency-audit.yml' that runs on PR open.
Configure Dependabot or Renovate to auto-raise PRs for security patches.
Test that PRs with vulnerable dependencies are blocked.
Run a simulated vulnerable dependency PR and verify audit fails.
acceptance_criteria: All dependencies are pinned to exact versions.
CI blocks PRs with HIGH/CRITICAL vulnerabilities.
Dependabot or Renovate is active and raises security PRs.
Simulated vulnerable dependency PR is blocked by CI.
